{{Quote|He's got that Corellian heart of gold...and silver...and spice.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]|<ref name="Dark Empire 3">[[Dark Empire 3|''Dark Empire'' 3]]</ref>}}
'''Shug Ninx''' was a [[Near-Human]] [[Outlaw tech/Legends|outlaw tech]] from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. On [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], Ninx owned and operated his famous [[Spacebarn]], an enormous [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] where [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] could repair and modify their [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. The only access to the Spacebarn was through a cleverly-disguised tunnel called [[The Chute]].

==Biography==
[[File:Shugninx.jpg|thumb|left|Ninx in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]]]
Ninx started out working for mechanic [[Bammy Decree/Legends|Bammy Decree]], and it is likely that he learnt the trade from the [[Human/Legends|Human]]. When helping Decree rebuild the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Stellar Envoy'']], Ninx sought out the fence Masel, who supplied him with a new [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brain]]. Unknown to Ninx, this was a ploy by Masel which resulted in the brain activating a cargo of buzz droids, which caused the explosion of an [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]]. Ninx would later do more work on the Envoy, years later when it was known as the ''Millennium Falcon''.

Ninx himself was a master mechanic, and probably could have found work in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had he not been a "half-breed", meaning he was the son of a Human and a nonhuman: although his father was a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]], his mother was one of the last [[Theelin/Legends|Theelin]]. This resulted in his mostly Human appearance except for blue and green mottling on his skin, and one thumb and two fingers on each of his hands. Given the prejudices of the Galactic Empire, he was fortunate to find work on Nar Shaddaa.

The Spacebarn, a [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] owned and operated by Ninx, was located near the [[Corellian Sector (Nar Shaddaa)/Legends|Corellian Sector]]. He made fast friends with many smugglers, including [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[Salla Zend]], [[Roa]], and [[Mako Spince]] among many others. For a time, he worked alongside a [[Tintinna/Legends|Tintinna]] named [[Kit (Tintinna)|Kit]]. Ninx's only rules in the Spacebarn were:

*Anything was available to use, so long as whoever used it put it back or compensated for damage.
*Pay promptly for the use of his or his techs' services.
*No intoxicating substances of any kind were ever allowed.

Given Ninx's amiable personality and skill as a mechanic, he was quite popular. He was very involved with the defensive planning prior to the [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|Battle of Nar Shaddaa]], and helped many smugglers outfit their ships with additional weaponry and shields. After the battle when Han Solo left, Ninx was one of the few who did not have a grudge against Solo.

When [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] returned in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body, Han and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] returned to Nar Shaddaa and met Ninx, who helped repair the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] in the Spacebarn. Violence erupted in the Spacebarn with the arrival of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], and Solo collaterally destroyed one of Ninx's prized ships during his escape. When Solo left Nar Shaddaa this time, Ninx did indeed have a sour taste in his mouth.
